mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates lower discount rates and corporate financing costs, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no significant positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rates exceeding 5.0 percent indicate severe labor market deterioration and collapsing consumer spending, triggering negative equity returns within one month.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 65% of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or out-of-sample hit rate falls below 40%.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent expansion in real GDP and real sales demonstrates synchronized aggregate demand growth, which amplifies corporate profit margins and drives positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: Joint signal yields a Rank IC below 0.04 over rolling 5-year windows, or positive returns occur in < 50% of co-expansion periods.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a post-shock recovery phase reflects rapid capacity utilization rebound and supply chain normalization, generating strong short-term positive equity momentum.
failure_condition: Signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.03) outside post-shock recovery regimes, or 5-day forward returns are not significantly positive during recovery phases.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price index readings above 220.0 signal accelerating inflation that forces central banks to tighten policy, compressing equity valuations and producing negative forward returns.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive in > 60% of high-inflation trigger months, or the signal shows no statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding labor demand and rising household income, which supports consumer spending and generates positive short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show no positive correlation with payroll growth, or Rank IC stays below 0.02 across all rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between housing starts and consumer prices, where housing starts rise while inflation remains subdued, indicates sustainable demand growth that supports positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: The interaction term yields a Rank IC below 0.03, or the joint condition fails to predict positive returns more than 50% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a late-cycle expansion signals capacity constraints and supply bottlenecks that erode profit margins, leading to negative equity returns over one month.
failure_condition: Signal produces positive returns in > 65% of late-cycle decline instances, or regime-conditional predictive power vanishes (Rank IC < 0.02).
